notes: I basically followed the redmcg guide found on GitHub.  The first time the game loaded, I played for a few hours looking for problems; the experience was flawless playing using default settings w/T.16000M+Throttle. During the same session, I  changed to full screen and tweaked the graphics settings (increasing details) and still didn't encounter any noticeable problems.  The next day, after entering the game world (after selecting Open), all input devices were unresponsive within the game (No keyboard, no HOTAS).  During my third test session, the input devices worked. (No pattern found yet why this happens.)  This time, I observed a graphics glitch on the main menu; the background was mostly black. The game ran fine after that.   Performance was always very good. Typically 90-240 Hz FPS. (I later  set the FPS limit to 144 Hz).  One issue I'm encountering is that after I quit the game (including the ED launcher), wine and a few other processes are still running.

notes:
The game has several issues that impact normal play. First I installed Protontricks and used that to install dotnet40 and vcrun2015 as others have recommended. The first time I clicked "Play" in Steam, nothing happened. The second time, the launcher froze and was unresponsive. The third time, the launcher worked, but logging in through the browser did not work. 

I then launched the game and had to fiddle with the resolution options to get it to display properly. I was also unable to open the Controls menu. I went into a training map and I was told to "Use [Not Bound] and [Not Bound]" to do something. Literally no buttons worked, I couldn't even use Esc to pause, so I just had to Alt+F4 and close the game. 

So I opened the launcher for a 4th time, the game runs and I'm able to edit controls unlike last time, I open up a training map and I noticed that the performance was quite bad. I can get over 60 FPS on High on Windows but struggle to get 30 on Low in Proton. There are also severe spikes when loading into and moving around complex areas like the "City" stations which makes the game feel like it's running at 5 FPS sometimes.

notes:
Following the instructions from https://github.com/redmcg/wine/wiki#the-official-build-of-proton-42-runs-ed  , creating the WINEPREFIX manually instead of allowing Steam to create the WINEPREFIX, made the game work. 

The shader compilation stage takes a long time in the first time. The window saying the game crashed appears but ignoring it allows all shaders to compile. Restarting the game it works.

To avoid out of memory crashes, a DXVK configuration file can be used, but some graphical glitches can appear (some blinking and low quality textures).

Performance-wise, it can keep 30 FPS most of the time with the Low preset using 1280x720 windowed. The game is pretty responsive: the galaxy map opens quickly, for example, which isn't the case on Windows.

It's not possible to activate the Horizons DLC using Proton: the COPY button from the launcher doesn't work, so it's not possible to get the code to activate on Frontier's site. After activating the code from a Windows computer, it's possible to start Elite Dangeours: Horizons but then it crashes on shader compilation stage at around 19% currently, closing the game in the process.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes:
I also followed the instructions on this wiki https://github.com/redmcg/wine/wiki and I also have been able to get the game to run perfectly, no need to launch from terminal.

I'm using Proton 4.2-9 and wine-4.11 (Staging) (installed manually using the wine repository, as informed on wine site download page.

Following https://github.com/redmcg/wine/wiki, play close attention to the necessity of removing (or not instaling if in a first-run) the wine-mono. It is necessary to set the WINEPREFIX pointing to compatdata of Elite, remove any files and folders there and then winetricks -q dotnet40 win7

It seems Vulkan needs to be ok also, before all the steps, but I already had that OK.

When starting Elite, it will take some time and Elite will install the redistributables and some DirectX stuff.

Enjoy!

notes:
Runs with upwards of 144 FPS on high settings! Followed the instructions other have here: 
>>> 
https://github.com/redmcg/wine/wiki
 <<<

Specifically:
(1) Install winetricks through your package manager
(1.5) Set a STEAMAPPS variable in the terminal for the next commands by finding the full path to your steamapps directory. On my system, that's /home/yours_truly/.local/share/Steam/steamapps.
(2) So I ran: STEAMAPPS=/home/yours_truly/.local/share/Steam/steamapps
(3) Run: export WINEPREFIX=$STEAMAPPS/compatdata/359320/pfx
(4) Run: rm -rf $WINEPREFIX
(5) Install dotnet40 through winetricks by running: winetricks -q dotnet40 win7
(6) And then launch Elite via Steam and just click past the error pop-ups before the launcher

notes: I followed the instructions at https://github.com/redmcg/wine/wiki. After messing around a bit with my joystick input (also on that website), I got the game running perfectly. I am currently using it with virtual joystick drivers from opentrack for head tracking (which it will occasionally forget the keybinds for - not sure why) and EDMC, also in Wine. You may need to manually create the Journal folder in `~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/359320/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Saved Games/Frontier Developments/Elite Dangerous` in order to get the game to save Journal files. If you are using a program, such as OBS, that uses hardware-accelerated video render, or the experimental Valve mesa renderer, be careful landing on planets. Occasionally the terrain generator can glitch out. (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qomwGVRIMzo) I'm not sure which caused it though. Otherwise, the game runs perfectly, with near-native performance. I went to Beagle Point and back on Linux.

title: Elite Dangerous

notes: You'll need to install an extra thing to get elite dangerous up and running, don't worry, its simple, just copy and paste. Look into getting protontricks installed. Now, when running elite for the first time at all it will silently crash, this is expected, we still need to do this at least once so that the prefix is generated, allowing protontricks to mess with it. Once that is done, open a terminal and type in "protontricks 359320 -q dotnet472 win7". Let it run and ignore any errors. Once its finished the game will be able to launch! Now, you'll notice similar commands such as the one I gave you, except its "dotnet40". The long story short of it is, if you use "dotnet472" you'll get more performance then just with "dotnet40", in my case 5-10 fps more. No idea how or why, it just does, and if your still concerned, its what the lutris installer for elite dangerous installs. Now, in the event you've already installed "dotnet40" and want to upgrade to "dotnet472" the procedure is simple as well. We're just going to delete the prefix you have right now and make a new one. So type in the terminal "protontricks --gui" and select elite dangerous. Press ok to any warnings. Then make sure that "Select the default wineprefix" is selected and hit ok. Then select "Delete ALL DATA AND APPLICATIONS INSIDE THIS WINEPREFIX" and hit ok and say yes. Once thats done, launch elite again, it will silently crash like before but it will regenerate the wine prefix. Then run "protontricks 359320 -q dotnet472 win7" like before. Enjoy!

notes:
I found a good solution for all .net problems, it's pretty simple.
According to that thread : https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=26757 you have to apply registry fix for your wineprefix (WINEPREFIX=your/path/to/prefix/in/compatdata/pfx wine reg add "HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Component Based Servicing\\Version" /v 6.1.7601.17592 /t REG_SZ /d "foo" /f).
Elite dangerous works perfectly after that.
After that most of the problems with installing and using dotnet (even 4.7.2) are solving by itself, only when launching game some errors appearing , but you just need to skip them and all will be good
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: I'm using a special sauce version of proton that has it running on wine 4.13 as base as opposed to wine 4.11-2 which can be found here: https://github.com/imaami/Proton/releases . In my experience it runs and feels better compared to 4.2-9 and proton 4.11-2! One issue I have though is that if you alt tab too many times, the mouse gets delayed and you'll need to restart your entire game to fix it. Now, you still need dotnet40 to play the game (dotnet472 keeps failing for me for some reason), but the proccess to do so is a bit trickier since protontricks seems to fail to support it. Your command will look something like this "WINE=$proton-i/dist/bin/wine WINEPREFIX=$Elite dangerous prefix winetricks -q dotnet40 win7" So in my case, it is "WINE=/.steam/steam/compatibilitytools.d/Proton-i-4.13-3/dist/bin/wine WINEPREFIX=/mnt/Pop!_OS Storage/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/359320/pfx winetricks -q dotnet40 win7" It's worth it for me though, I like the improvements in frametimes and mouse issues.

notes: The game need .NET 4.0 and DXVK in order to run. It can be achieved using protontricks. Run the game for the first time and let it install some library via steam. Then run "protontricks 35920 uninstaller" and uninstall Windows Mono Support. You can now install .net 4.0 and dxvk via protontricks with "protontricks 35920 win7 dxvk dotnet40" it should get the things done. If not you can try the protontricks uninstaller again and delete everything then retry launching it via steam, try reinstalling the whole game too... Otherwise, it runs smoothly in space but struggle in stations. It's an expected behavior with my CG I notice the same on Windows

notes:
Won't work straight out of the box, on non of the Proton versions. I managed to get Elite Dangerous to work on this version using the tweaks provided by others: 

1. Install Matokings' protontricks from Github with the pipx guide.
2. Run 'protontricks 359320 -q dotnet40 win7' in a terminal after installing protontricks. (This will install .NET 4.0 and sets the prefix to Windows 7 for Elite Dangerous).
3. Run the game as you would normally through Steam on Proton 4.11-7. Works for both ED and ED Horizons.
